description:
# Why

`bl remaster --detach` (the warm path, `remaster::detach`) re-roots the
`balls/tasks` orphan inside `store.state_worktree_dir()` ŌĆö which, while
`master_url` is still set, resolves to `.balls/state-repo`. It then clears
`master_url` from config. But every subsequent `Store::discover` resolves the
layout fresh: with `master_url` gone, `state_worktree_for` returns
`.balls/worktree` ŌĆö a path the detach never created or migrated to.

Confirmed 2026-05-20 with a `bl` built from main:

- standalone-init -> `remaster --commit <url>` -> `remaster --detach`:
  `.balls/worktree` still exists as a stale pre-flip leftover, so `discover`
  silently reads THAT branch. Tasks created while federated live on the
  re-rooted `.balls/state-repo` branch and go invisible ŌĆö `bl prime` shows an
  empty Ready list. Silent data divergence.
- seeded-federated `bl init` -> `remaster --detach`: `.balls/worktree` never
  existed, so `discover` hard-fails ('task state is missing
  .balls/worktree/.balls/tasks'). The repo is bricked until `bl repair`.

The cold path (`try_cold_detach`, bl-dcd3) gets this right ŌĆö it re-inits
`.balls/worktree` explicitly. The warm path does not.

# Scope

- After `remaster::detach` re-roots the orphan, leave the post-detach layout
  as the one `discover` will resolve: migrate the re-rooted branch into
  `.balls/worktree` (mirroring `try_cold_detach`'s `setup_state_branch`), or
  run detach against `.balls/worktree` from the start.
- A standalone repo with a stale `.balls/worktree` must not be left reading it
  instead of the detached state.
- Test: warm detach of (a) a standalone-then-flipped repo and (b) a
  seeded-federated repo ŌĆö a `bl prime`/`bl create` after detach must see the
  detached task set, not error and not silently read a stale branch.

# Relation

Composes with bl-82a4 (symlink-based config symmetry); if that lands a single
canonical state path this collapses. But it is an independently-shippable
correctness bug (a bricked repo) and should not wait.

# Origin

Found while delivering bl-ebae (federated-flip git hygiene), 2026-05-20.
bl-ebae's detach test only checks .gitignore/git-status shape, not a
post-detach lifecycle command, so it did not surface this. Out of bl-ebae's
scope ŌĆö that ball is purely git hygiene.
title: Warm bl remaster --detach leaves the repo on the wrong state worktree
